title: Implement PLAN step A foundations

title: Run strict Step A validation on target DGX host
description: Execute tests/A1_host_prereqs.sh and ./agent test A without skip flags on the target DGX host (root context as needed), then archive outputs in docs/runbooks.
close_reason: Validated on target host with sudo ./agent test A (A1/A2/A3 green)

title: Run Step I ComfyUI integration on target DGX host
description: Validate ComfyUI end-to-end on the DGX host with at least one checkpoint model present. Run tests/I1_comfyui.sh in strict mode (AGENTIC_COMFY_STRICT_SMOKE=1), confirm generated artifact persistence under /srv/agentic/comfyui/output, and document GPU/runtime caveats in docs/runbooks.

comments.author: vuissoz
comments.text: Implemented Plan I code updates in commit cbda089: ComfyUI now requests GPU (gpus: all), carries low-priority marker (AGENTIC_GPU_PROFILE=lowprio), uses read-only rootfs/tmpfs, and I1 test now validates GPU wiring plus proxy-path enforcement. Remaining follow-up: run strict integration on DGX host with real checkpoint (AGENTIC_COMFY_STRICT_SMOKE=1).

title: Fix failing rootless-dev tests (J2, K1, K3)
description:
Context
- Dedicated tracking issue for failing tests in `AGENTIC_PROFILE=rootless-dev`.
- Scope is test reliability in rootless mode only (separate from strict-prod validation).

Failing tests (latest run)
1. `tests/J2_rag_smoke.sh`
- Failure: `HTTP 404 on http://<ollama-gate-ip>:11435/v1/embeddings`.
- Expected: embeddings endpoint available through gate for RAG ingest/query flow.

2. `tests/K1_openclaw.sh`
- Failure: `optional-openclaw` did not become ready within timeout (container restarting/unhealthy).
- Expected: service healthy and test assertions on auth/allowlist/audit pass.

3. `tests/K3_portainer.sh`
- Failure: loopback probe fails with connection reset (`curl: (56) Recv failure: Connection reset by peer`).
- Expected: optional portainer reachable on configured loopback port and security assertions pass.

Acceptance criteria
- `AGENTIC_PROFILE=rootless-dev ./agent test J` passes.
- `AGENTIC_PROFILE=rootless-dev ./agent test K` passes for K1/K3 (or explicit documented skip policy if intentionally unsupported in rootless mode).
- `./agent doctor` remains green in rootless-dev after fixes.
- Failure signatures above are no longer reproducible.

Notes
- Coordinate with umbrella issue `dgx-spark-agentic-stack-de9` for cross-cutting gate/routing changes.
acceptance_criteria: Rootless-dev failing tests are green or explicitly documented/skipped with rationale and updated tests

title: Run full Step F tests on DGX host with /srv/agentic permissions
description: Execute tests/F1_agent_cli.sh, tests/F2_update_rollback.sh, tests/F3_doctor.sh on target host where core+agents are deployed and DOCKER-USER policy is active. Capture artifacts and close if all pass.

title: Run Step H integration on target host (OpenWebUI/OpenHands)
description: Deploy UI stack with 'agent up ui' and execute tests/H1_openwebui.sh and tests/H2_openhands.sh. Confirm auth behavior, loopback binds, no docker.sock on OpenHands, and gate log proof.

comments.author: vuissoz
comments.text: Progress update (2026-02-19): rootless-dev validation now passes for Step H smoke tests after OpenWebUI permission + dual-network port-publish fix. Commands: tests/H1_openwebui.sh and tests/H2_openhands.sh both green with AGENTIC_PROFILE=rootless-dev. Remaining blocker for full stack validation is Step G obs restart loop due legacy volume ownership (tracked in dgx-spark-agentic-stack-vma).

title: Validate strict-prod sudo host-net rollback flow end-to-end (agent net apply + rollback host-net + B5) on a host with /srv/agentic writable and iptables access
Run Step G observability integration on target DGX host
description: Deploy obs stack with 'agent up obs', then run tests/G1_obs_up.sh with DCGM enabled. Validate Prometheus targets, Grafana loopback bind, Loki ingest/query, and GPU metrics scrape.

title: Close remaining PLAN.md implementation gaps
description:
Context
- Track remaining PLAN.md items that are not implemented or not passing on current rootless-dev validation.
- This umbrella issue should stay open until all listed gaps are either implemented and green, or intentionally descoped with ADR updates.

Current gaps observed
1. D1 gate embeddings compatibility
- `POST /v1/embeddings` is required by PLAN.md and Step J RAG flow.
- Current runtime behavior observed during tests: endpoint returns 404 in the active gate container.

2. J2 RAG smoke end-to-end
- `tests/J2_rag_smoke.sh` fails in current rootless-dev run due to embeddings path incompatibility.
- Need ingestion/query green with deterministic repeatability criteria from PLAN.md.

3. K optional modules reliability
- `tests/K1_openclaw.sh` fails (container unhealthy/restarting).
- `tests/K3_portainer.sh` fails (loopback endpoint reset/unreachable in test).
- K acceptance remains incomplete until K1/K3 are green in supported profile(s).

4. Release traceability warning
- `agent doctor` warns active release manifest missing (`deployments/current/images.json`).
- PLAN/F2 traceability requires consistent release snapshot artifacts.

5. Newly added PLAN scope: TRT-LLM + NVFP4 routing via gate
- C3: optional `trtllm` service/profile not implemented yet.
- D1/D4: model-based backend routing policy (`ollama` vs `trtllm`) not implemented yet.
- Need explicit backend audit fields and deterministic failure behavior when routed backend is unavailable.

Definition of done
- D1: `/v1/embeddings` available via gate (no 404).
- J2: `tests/J2_rag_smoke.sh` passes.
- K1/K3: tests pass under declared profile requirements.
- F2/F3 traceability warning resolved with release manifests present.
- C3/D4 implemented with tests (`C3_trtllm_basic`, `D4_gate_backend_routing`) and docs/runbook updates.
acceptance_criteria: All listed gaps implemented and validated by passing tests and doctor with no unresolved blockers

title: Run Step J RAG smoke with real embeddings model
description: Execute tests/J1_qdrant.sh and tests/J2_rag_smoke.sh on target host with RAG_GATE_DRY_RUN=0 and a local embeddings model available in Ollama. Confirm ingestion/query behavior and record model choice + runtime in docs/runbooks.

Context: local dev environment cannot apply iptables DOCKER-USER rules without sudo.

Remaining work:
- run './agent up core' with root privileges on DGX host,
- verify tests/B4_docker_user_enforced.sh passes,
- capture 'iptables -S DOCKER-USER' and counter increase evidence in runbook.
close_reason: Validated on DGX host: sudo ./agent up core, sudo ./agent test B (B1-B4 green), sudo ./agent doctor READY on 2026-02-09.

title: Clarify mvance/unbound digest pinning for rollback
description:
Le 9 fevrier 2026, verification locale de `docker manifest inspect --verbose mvance/unbound:latest`:

- Le tag `mvance/unbound:latest` retourne un manifest Docker V2 simple (`application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json`), pas un index multi-arch.
- Le digest a pinner pour un deploiement reproductible est le digest du descriptor/manifest: `sha256:76906da36d1806f3387338f15dcf8b357c51ce6897fb6450d6ce010460927e90`.
- Le digest `sha256:a7778ebb14b25328fc6de5b58ddf5dfe3e0891afec8fd86744995fd8dbcd5b4e` est `config.digest`; il ne doit pas etre utilise pour le pinning d'image Compose.
- Exemple correct:
  `image: mvance/unbound@sha256:76906da36d1806f3387338f15dcf8b357c51ce6897fb6450d6ce010460927e90`
- Contrainte plateforme observee: `linux/amd64`.

Impact CDC:
- `agent update`/snapshot digests doit stocker le digest de manifest deploye, pas le config digest.
- `agent rollback` doit repinner exactement ce manifest digest.
close_reason: Step B switched to multi-arch unbound image (klutchell/unbound); mvance-specific digest pinning is no longer applicable.

title: Harden ollama-gate test mode before production rollout
description: Step D enables GATE_ENABLE_TEST_MODE=1 in compose.core for deterministic queue/sticky tests. Add a production-safe toggle strategy (default off in runtime), and adapt tests to enable it only in dedicated test profile.

title: Implement optional modules K1-K3 behind hardened profiles
description: Add optional modules for K1 (Clawdbot), K2 (MCP catalog), and K3 (Portainer) with disabled-by-default profiles, secret handling, and dedicated compliance tests. Ensure no docker.sock direct mount and keep loopback-only binds.

title: Harden Ollama egress after Step C dual-network publish
description: Step C attaches ollama to both agentic and agentic-egress so host loopback publish works while agentic remains internal. Add explicit DOCKER-USER rules that constrain ollama outbound traffic without breaking proxy/unbound, and extend doctor checks accordingly.

title: Run E2 confinement test on deployed core+agents stack
description: Prerequisite: run 'agent up core,agents' on target host, then execute tests/E2_agents_confinement.sh and collect results in test reports.

title: Validate Step J against full core stack with healthy ollama
description: Run ./agent up core,rag and ./agent test J in target DGX environment where ollama is healthy; confirm no regressions without stubs.

title: Fix GitHub access blocked by SSH password prompt
description:
Context
- Git operations to GitHub are blocked/interrupted because remote access currently requires interactive SSH password authentication.
- This affects non-interactive workflows (agent automation, CI-like runs, and scripted `git pull --rebase` / `git push`).

Problem
- GitHub connection is behind SSH with password prompt.
- Automation cannot complete end-to-end publish/sync steps when credentials require manual prompt handling.

Impact
- `git push` and sync steps are unreliable in automated sessions.
- Work may remain stranded locally if push cannot complete unattended.

Proposed safe remediation
1. Prefer SSH key-based auth with `ssh-agent` (no password prompt during session).
2. If organization policy requires passphrase, preload agent once per session.
3. As fallback, use HTTPS remote with PAT via credential helper (non-interactive).

Acceptance criteria
- `git ls-remote origin` succeeds non-interactively.
- `git pull --rebase` and `git push` succeed without password prompt in the target workflow context.
- Runbook updated with the chosen auth method and recovery steps.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub pull/push operations work non-interactively in normal repo workflow

title: Ensure active release snapshot is present after profile-based deploys
description:
Context:
`agent doctor` in rootless-dev warns that `${AGENTIC_ROOT}/deployments/current/images.json` is missing even when stack services are running.

Need:
- clarify intended workflow (`agent up` vs `agent update`) and ensure release metadata is generated consistently for running stacks.
- avoid false-negative compliance drift when deployments are healthy but no active snapshot is linked.
acceptance_criteria:
- Document expected snapshot lifecycle
- Implement script/path fix so `deployments/current/images.json` exists after supported deploy flow
- `agent doctor` no longer warns for a healthy, snapshot-backed deployment

title: Fix rootless-dev observability restart loop caused by legacy volume ownership
description:
Context:
After recreating obs services in rootless-dev, Prometheus/Grafana/Loki/Promtail can enter restart loops when existing runtime files are owned by root from prior strict-prod runs.

Observed errors:
- Prometheus: open /prometheus/queries.active: permission denied
- Grafana: GF_PATHS_DATA=/var/lib/grafana is not writable
- Loki: stat /loki/rules: permission denied
- Promtail: open /promtail/positions/positions.yaml: permission denied

Expected:
- rootless-dev runtime init should detect/migrate incompatible ownership safely or fail fast with actionable remediation.
- `agent up obs` should not leave services in restart loops due to silent ownership drift.
acceptance_criteria:
- Repro documented with exact command sequence
- Rootless-safe remediation implemented (no broad permission weakening)
- `agent up obs` + `tests/G1_obs_up.sh` pass in rootless-dev after fix
- Strict-prod security posture remains unchanged

title: Remove legacy 'clawdbot' references and standardize on OpenClaw
description:
Context
- Ensure the project uses `openclaw` consistently and removes all residual `clawdbot` references.
- Current runtime failure indicates stale behavior where container expects `clawdbot` mode while compose invokes `openclaw`.

Problem
- Optional OpenClaw container can restart with:
  - `invalid choice: 'openclaw' (choose from clawdbot, mcp)`
- This suggests legacy naming remains in built artifacts or scripts not aligned with repository naming.

Scope
1. Remove/replace any `clawdbot` references in repository sources, scripts, docs, tests, and templates.
2. Ensure optional module image build path uses updated code (no stale cached artifact mismatch).
3. Keep naming canonical as `openclaw` for mode/service/profile labels.

Acceptance criteria
- `rg -n "clawdbot" -S .` returns no active references (except intentionally documented migration notes, if any).
- `optional-openclaw` starts with `command: openclaw` and stays healthy.
- `tests/K1_openclaw.sh` no longer fails with mode-choice mismatch.
- Runbook/docs mention only `OpenClaw` naming for active runtime paths.

Notes
- Coordinate with `dgx-spark-agentic-stack-4ik` (rootless failing tests) and `dgx-spark-agentic-stack-de9` (PLAN gaps umbrella).
acceptance_criteria: No residual clawdbot references in active codepaths; OpenClaw runtime and K1 test work with canonical naming
